tasks.subject: status-bar-inline-management [P2] Add configure icon and right-click context menus to ChatStatusSection
tasks.description:
Add the trailing configure icon to the status bar and wrap each item with a right-click context menu. Also add a background context menu on the status bar container. ## Modifications to `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatStatusSection.tsx` ### 1. Add Configure Icon Import the configure popover and the `SlidersHorizontal` icon: ```tsx import { SlidersHorizontal } from 'lucide-react'; import { StatusBarConfigurePopover, STATUS_BAR_REGISTRY, resetStatusBarPreferences, } from '@/layers/features/status'; import { Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Add the configure icon as the last `StatusLine.Item` in the `statusLineContent` JSX, after the `clients` item and before the closing `` tag: ```tsx

Configure status bar

 ``` The configure icon is always visible (`visible={true}`) and cannot be hidden. This ensures the status bar container never fully collapses — `hasVisibleChildren` in `StatusLine` will always be true, providing a persistent inline entry point. ### 2. Add Right-Click Context Menus Import `ContextMenu` components: ```tsx import { ContextMenu, ContextMenuTrigger, ContextMenuContent, ContextMenuItem, ContextMenuSeparator, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Create a helper component to wrap each item with its context menu. This avoids duplicating context menu JSX for every item: ```tsx interface ItemContextMenuProps { /** The item label from the registry, e.g. "Git Status". Null for non-registry items. */ itemLabel: string | null; /** Callback to hide this specific item. Null for non-registry items. */ onHide: (() => void) | null; /** Callback to open the configure popover. */ onConfigure: () => void; children: React.ReactNode; } function ItemContextMenu({ itemLabel, onHide, onConfigure, children }: ItemContextMenuProps) { return ( {children} {itemLabel && onHide && ( <> Hide "{itemLabel}" )} Configure status bar... Reset to defaults ); } ``` Wrap each `StatusLine.Item`'s children with `ItemContextMenu`. For example, the CWD item changes from: ```tsx {status.cwd && } ``` to: ```tsx setShowStatusBarCwd(false)} onConfigure={() => setConfigureOpen(true)} > {status.cwd && } ``` Repeat for all registry items (git, permission, model, cost, context, sound, sync, polling, tunnel, version). The `connection` and `clients` items are system-managed and do NOT get context menus with "Hide" — they only get the background menu. The configure icon itself does NOT get a context menu (it's not a toggleable item). ### 3. Background Context Menu Wrap the entire `statusLineContent` variable with a background context menu. This provides "Configure status bar..." and "Reset to defaults" when right-clicking the status bar background (not on a specific item): Add state for controlling the configure popover: ```tsx const [configureOpen, setConfigureOpen] = useState(false); ``` Pass `open` and `onOpenChange` to the `StatusBarConfigurePopover` on the configure icon so it can be opened programmatically from context menus. ### 4. Look up labels from registry Instead of hardcoding label strings, look them up from the registry: ```tsx const getItemLabel = (key: string): string | null => { const item = STATUS_BAR_REGISTRY.find((r) => r.key === key); return item?.label ?? null; }; ``` ## Acceptance Criteria 1. A `SlidersHorizontal` icon appears as the last item in the status bar, separated by a middot 2. The icon has `text-muted-foreground/50` default opacity, brightens on hover 3. Clicking the icon opens the configure popover (upward on desktop, sheet on mobile) 4. The icon has a tooltip reading "Configure status bar" 5. Right-clicking any registry item shows: "Hide [label]", separator, "Configure status bar...", "Reset to defaults" 6. Right-clicking the status bar background (not on an item) shows: "Configure status bar...", "Reset to defaults" 7. "Hide [label]" sets the corresponding `showStatusBar*` to false, hiding the item 8. "Configure status bar..." opens the configure popover 9. "Reset to defaults" calls `resetStatusBarPreferences()` 10. System-managed items (connection, clients) do not have "Hide" in their context menu 11. The configure icon cannot be hidden (always `visible={true}`) ## Tests Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/__tests__/ChatStatusSection-configure.test.tsx`: - Test that the configure icon renders with correct aria-label - Test that clicking the configure icon opens the popover - Test that the configure icon is always visible even when all other items are hidden - Test that right-clicking a registry item shows the context menu with "Hide [label]" - Test that "Hide" menu item calls the correct store setter with false
tasks.activeForm: Adding configure icon and context menus to ChatStatusSection

tasks.subject: status-bar-inline-management [P3] Refactor Settings dialog Status Bar tab to use registry
tasks.description:
Refactor the Status Bar tab in `SettingsDialog.tsx` to iterate the registry instead of hardcoding 10 individual `SettingRow` + `Switch` blocks. This ensures the Settings dialog and popover always show the same items with the same labels and descriptions. ## Modifications to `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx` ### 1. Import registry and hook ```tsx import { STATUS_BAR_REGISTRY, getGroupedRegistryItems, useStatusBarVisibility, resetStatusBarPreferences, } from '@/layers/features/status'; import type { StatusBarItemConfig } from '@/layers/features/status'; ``` ### 2. Create a reusable toggle row component Define inside the file or extract to a small helper: ```tsx function StatusBarSettingRow({ item }: { item: StatusBarItemConfig }) { const [visible, setVisible] = useStatusBarVisibility(item.key); return ( ); } ``` ### 3. Replace the hardcoded Status Bar panel Replace the current `NavigationLayoutPanel value="statusBar"` content (lines ~301-384 in the current file) with: ```tsx
Reset to defaults } > Status Bar {STATUS_BAR_REGISTRY.map((item) => ( ))}
``` This replaces the ~80 lines of hardcoded SettingRow blocks with a 10-line map over the registry. ### 4. Remove unused store destructuring The following properties can be removed from the `useAppStore()` destructuring in `SettingsDialog` since they're now accessed via `useStatusBarVisibility` inside `StatusBarSettingRow`: ```ts // REMOVE these from the useAppStore() destructuring: showStatusBarCwd, setShowStatusBarCwd, showStatusBarPermission, setShowStatusBarPermission, showStatusBarModel, setShowStatusBarModel, showStatusBarCost, setShowStatusBarCost, showStatusBarContext, setShowStatusBarContext, showStatusBarGit, setShowStatusBarGit, showStatusBarSound, setShowStatusBarSound, showStatusBarSync, setShowStatusBarSync, showStatusBarPolling, setShowStatusBarPolling, showStatusBarTunnel, setShowStatusBarTunnel, ``` ### 5. Add "Reset to defaults" to the Status Bar panel header The panel header now includes a "Reset to defaults" button that calls `resetStatusBarPreferences()` (the scoped reset), NOT the global `resetPreferences()`. The Appearance tab keeps its own reset button that calls the global `resetPreferences()` — these are intentionally separate. ## Acceptance Criteria 1. The Status Bar tab renders exactly the same 11 toggle rows as before, with labels and descriptions matching the registry 2. Toggling a switch updates the same Zustand store property as before 3. The "Reset to defaults" button in the Status Bar tab calls `resetStatusBarPreferences()` (scoped), not `resetPreferences()` (global) 4. The Appearance tab's reset button still calls the global `resetPreferences()` 5. No individual `showStatusBar*` / `setShowStatusBar*` properties are destructured from `useAppStore()` in `SettingsDialog` (they're accessed via `useStatusBarVisibility` in the child component) 6. The Status Bar tab is not missing the `showStatusBarVersion` toggle (currently missing from the hardcoded version — the registry includes it) ## Tests Update existing Settings dialog tests (if any exist) or create `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/SettingsDialog-statusbar.test.tsx`: - Test that the Status Bar tab renders all 11 registry items as toggle rows - Test that toggling a switch calls the store setter - Test that "Reset to defaults" in the Status Bar tab calls the scoped reset (verify only status bar prefs change) - Test that the Appearance tab reset still uses the global reset
tasks.activeForm: Refactoring Settings dialog Status Bar tab to use registry
